volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 2.3.313 - Section 2.4.363b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 2.3.313
sections.original: Sceledre , Sceledre , quis homo in terra te alter est audacior ?
sections.translation: Sceledrus , Sceledrus , what one man is there on earth more impudent than yourself ? Who more than yourself has been born with the Deities hostile and enraged ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.314b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.315
sections.original: Iuben tibi oculos exfodiri , quibus id quod nusquam est vides ?
sections.translation: Do you want those eyes of yours gouged out , with which you see what never existed ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.316
sections.translation: How , what never existed ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.316b
sections.original: Non ego tuam empsim vitam vitiosa nuce .
sections.translation: I would not buy your life at the price of a rotten nut .

sections.label: Section 2.3.317
sections.translation: Why , what ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.317b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter , do you ask ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.317c
sections.translation: And why shouldn ’ t I ask ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.318
sections.original: Non tu tibi istam praetruncari linguam largiloquam iubes ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you beg for that tongue of yours to be cut out , that prates so at random ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.319
sections.translation: Why should I beg for that ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.319b
sections.original: Philocomasium eccam domi , quam in proxumo
sections.translation: Why , Philocomasium is there at home , she whom

sections.label: Section 2.3.320
sections.original: vidisse aibas te osculantem atque amplexantem cum altero .
sections.translation: you were saying that you had seen next door kissing and toying with another man .

sections.label: Section 2.3.321
sections.original: Mirumst lolio victitare te tam vili tritico .
sections.translation: ’ Tis a wonder that you are in the habit of feeding on darnel [*] , with wheat at so low a price .

sections.label: Section 2.3.322
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.322b
sections.translation: Because you are so dim of sight .

sections.label: Section 2.3.322c
sections.translation: You gallows-bird , ’ tis you , indeed , that are blind , with a vengeance , and not dim of sight ; for , sure enough , there she is at home .

sections.label: Section 2.3.324
sections.translation: How ? At home ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.324b
sections.translation: At home , i ’ faith , undoubtedly .

sections.label: Section 2.3.324c
sections.translation: Be off with you ; you are playing with me , Palaestrio

sections.label: Section 2.3.325
sections.translation: My hands are dirty , then .

sections.label: Section 2.3.325b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.325c
sections.translation: Because I am playing with dirt .

sections.label: Section 2.3.326
sections.translation: A mischief on your head .

sections.label: Section 2.3.326b
sections.translation: Nay rather , Sceledrus , it shall be on yours , I promise you , unless you change for fresh your eyes and your talk . But our door made a noise .

sections.label: Section 2.3.328b
sections.translation: Well , I shall watch here out of doors , for there is no way by which she can pass hence in-doors , except through the front door .

sections.label: Section 2.3.330
sections.original: Quin domi eccam . nescio quae te , Sceledre , scelera suscitant .
sections.translation: But there she is , at home . I don ’ t know , Scledrus , what mischief is possessing you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.331
sections.original: Mihi ego video , mihi ego sapio , mihi ego credo plurumum :
sections.translation: I see for my own self , I judge for my own self , I have especial faith in my own self : no man shall frighten me out of it , but that she is in that house . ( Points to the house of PERIPLECOMENUS .) Here I ’ ll take my stand , that she may not steal out home without my knowledge .

sections.label: Section 2.3.334
sections.original: Meus illic homo est , deturbabo iam ego illum de pugnaculis .
sections.translation: This fellow is in my hands ; now will I drive him from his strong hold .

sections.label: Section 2.3.335
sections.original: vin iam faciam , ut stultividum ⋯ fateare ?
sections.translation: ( To SCLEDRUS ) Do you wish me now to make you own that you don ’ t see correctly ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.335b
sections.translation: Come , do it then .

sections.label: Section 2.3.336
sections.original: Neque te quicquam sapere corde neque oculis uti ?
sections.translation: And that you neither think aright in your mind , nor yet make use of your eyes ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.336b
sections.translation: I ’ d have you do it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.337
sections.original: Nempe tu istíc ais esse erilem concubinam ?
sections.translation: Do you say , then that the lady of your master is there in that house ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.337b
sections.translation: I assert , as well , that I saw here here in this house ( points to the house of PERIPLECOMENUS ) , toying with a strange man .

sections.label: Section 2.3.339
sections.original: Scin tu nullum commeatum hínc esse a nobis ?
sections.translation: don ’ t you know that there is no communication between our house here and that one ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.339b
sections.translation: I know it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.340
sections.original: Neque solarium neque hortum , nisi per impluvium ?
sections.translation: Neither by the terrace [*] , nor by the garden , only through the skylight ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.340b
sections.translation: I know it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.341
sections.original: Quid nunc ? si ea domist , si facio , ut eam exire hinc videas domo ,
sections.translation: What then , if she is now at home ? If I shall make her , so as you may see her , come out hence from our house , are you not deserving of many a lashing ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.342b
sections.translation: I am so deserving .

sections.label: Section 2.3.342c
sections.translation: Watch that door , then , that she may not privily betake herself out thence without your knowledge and pass here into our house .

sections.label: Section 2.3.344
sections.translation: ’ Tis my intention to do so .

sections.label: Section 2.3.344b
sections.original: Pede ego iam illam huc tibi sistam in viam . —
sections.translation: Upon her feet [*] will I place her this moment here before you in the street .

sections.label: Section 2.3.345
sections.original: Agedum ergo face . vólo scire , utrum egon id quod vidi viderim
sections.translation: Come , then , and do so .

sections.label: Section 2.3.346
sections.original: an illic faciat , quod facturum dicit , ut ea sit domi .
sections.translation: I wish to know whether I did see that which I did see , [*] or whether he can do that which he says he can do — make her to be at home . For , really , I have eyes of my own , and I don ’ t ever ask to borrow them out of doors . But this fellow is forever fawning about her ; he is always near her ; he is called first to meat ; his mess is given [*] to him first .

sections.label: Section 2.3.350
sections.original: nam illic noster est fortasse circiter triennium ,
sections.translation: For this fellow has been , perhaps , about three years with us ; nor fares it better with any other servant in our family than with him . But it is necessary for me to mind what I am about ; to keep my eye upon this door . If I take my station here , this way , in faith , I warrant they will never impose on me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.354
sections.translation: Be sure to remember my instructions .

sections.label: Section 2.4.354b
sections.translation: It ’ s strange you should so often remind me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.355
sections.original: At metuo ut satis sis subdola .
sections.translation: But I fear you may not prove cunning enough .

sections.label: Section 2.4.355b
sections.translation: Give me even ten scholars , though far from artful , I could instruct them so as to prove artful ; in me alone is there a superabundance of artfulness ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.357
sections.original: Age nunciam insiste in dolos ; ego abs te procul recedam .
sections.translation: come , then , now put your plans in force ; I ’ ll step aside here .

sections.label: Section 2.4.358
sections.translation: What have you to say , Scledrus ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.358b
sections.original: Hanc rem gero . habeo auris , loquere quidvis .
sections.translation: I ’ m about this business of mine ; I have got ears , say what you please .

sections.label: Section 2.4.359
sections.original: Credo ego istoc extemplo tibi esse eundum actutum extra portam ,
sections.translation: I think that in that self-same position [*] you will have to die outside the gates ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.360
sections.translation: when , with hands outstretched , you will be carrying your cross .

sections.label: Section 2.4.360b
sections.translation: For what reason so ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.361
sections.original: Respice dum ad laevam : quis illaec est mulier ?
sections.translation: Just look on your left hand ; who is that lady ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.361b
sections.translation: O ye immortal Gods , it really is the lady of my master !

sections.label: Section 2.4.362b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , so she seems to me as well . Do then , now , since so you would have it —

sections.label: Section 2.4.363b
sections.translation: Do what ?
